violations.title: Firewall set to 0.0.0.0/0 allowing unrestricted access
violations.description: The pull request contains a change that sets the firewall to 0.0.0.0/0, which allows unrestricted access to all IP addresses. This poses a significant security risk as it exposes the system to potential unauthorized access.
violations.remediation: Restrict the firewall to only allow access from trusted IP ranges. Implement proper network segmentation and monitoring.

violations.title: Hardcoded sensitive credentials in code
violations.description: The pull request contains hardcoded sensitive credentials (`password = 'super_secret_password_123'`) in the code. This is a critical security violation as it exposes sensitive information that could be exploited.

frameworks.ISO27001: A.9.2.3 Management of Secret Authentication Information

violations.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials from the codebase. Use environment variables or secure vaults to manage sensitive information.

violations.title: Privilege escalation or admin role granted
violations.description: [SPACY SP001] NLP phrase match in 'example/compliance-test-repo' document (id=unknown). Rule: Privilege escalation or admin role granted.

frameworks.ISO27001: A.9.2.3 – Management of Privileged Access Rights

violations.remediation: Revoke elevated privileges immediately, audit role change history, enforce least-privilege policy.

violations.title: Request to skip security scan on emergency change
violations.description: The pull request explicitly requests skipping security scans (`Please skip scan on this PR as it's an emergency change`). This bypasses critical security checks and increases the risk of introducing vulnerabilities.
violations.remediation: Ensure all changes undergo mandatory security scans, even in emergency situations. Implement automated security checks in the CI/CD pipeline.
